Debug logging revealed DPoP verification failing with \"Invalid JWK component length\" then \"DPoP signature verification failed\". Root cause: DPoP tokens created with empty x/y values in JWK header, and DER-encoded signature was being base64url encoded directly instead of extracting raw r||s components first."}

nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"main","confidence":85,"prompt":"# Detailed Next Steps Plan

## Phase 12: Architecture Refinement (Priority: High)

### 12.1. Dependency Injection Cleanup (`XrpcMethodRegistry`)
The `XrpcMethodRegistry` currently accepts `PDSApplication` but still extracts `legacyController` to pass to helper functions. This enforces a dependency on the legacy controller.

- [ ] **Refactor `registerServerAccountAndSessionMethods`**
  - Change signature to accept `PDSAccountService` and `PDSConfiguration` directly instead of `PDSController`.
  - Update implementations to use extraction logic from services.
- [ ] **Refactor `extractDIDFromAuthHeader`**
  - Currently takes `PDSController` to access `serverHost` or `jwtMinter`.
  - Update to accept `PDSConfiguration` or `JWTMinter` directly.
- [ ] **Remove `application.legacyController` usage**
  - Identify all call sites in `registerMethodsWithDispatcher:application:`.
  - Replace with specific service properties from `PDSApplication` (e.g., `adminController`, `repoService`).

go"}

{"branch":"main","confidence":95,"prompt":"Implement the following plan:

# Production Readiness Review — ATProtoPDS (Small Self-Hoster)

## Context

Production readiness assessment and remediation plan for self-hosting the Objective-C ATProtoPDS implementation. The codebase is substantially complete (96/96 core AT Protocol methods, OAuth/DPoP, SQLite persistence, logging) but has several gaps. Scope: **single-machine small self-hoster**, not distributed deployment.

[Full plan omitted for brevity — see conversation transcript]

Fixes implemented:
C1: Wire Resend email provider into PDSApplication.m
C2: Enforce JWT issuer at startup (exit(1) if production + pds.local)
C3: Reverse proxy / HTTPS deployment documentation
H1: Content-Type header (was already present)
H2: Parse Resend error JSON body (name/message fields)
H3: Persist token invalidation to file (survives restarts)
H4: Warn on plain-text admin password at startup
H5: Warn on X-Admin-Token in production at startup
M1: Add 409 to retryable HTTP status codes
M2: Register NSUncaughtExceptionHandler (C function, not block)
M3: Apply WAL + performance PRAGMAs to service DB pools"}

Standalone AppView Server (Full Network, Native Backfill, Interest-Graph Partial Mode)
nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"main","confidence":90,"prompt":"# Standalone AppView Server for Garazyk (Full Network, Native Backfill, Interest-Graph Partial Mode)

## Summary
- Build a standalone AppView service in this repo, with PDS running in proxy mode to that service.
- Use `subscribeRepos` as canonical realtime ingest and native `sync.getRepo` backfill for completeness and recovery.
- Keep one global ingest stream and add relevance-based partial materialization (Konbini-style), while preserving a path to future true sharding.
- Research-informed design choices:
- [HappyView](https://github.com/gamesgamesgamesgamesgames/happyview) and [Tap README](https://github.com/bluesky-social/indigo/blob/main/cmd/tap/README.md): decoupled ingest/materialize shape is good, but v1 should not depend on an external Tap runtime.
- [Quickslice](https://tangled.org/slices.network/quickslice): adopt native `getRepo` backfill concurrency/rate-limit controls and restart-safe state.
- [Parakeet](https://tangled.org/parakeet.at/parakeet): adopt repo backfill state machine (`dirty/processing/synced`) and merge queued live deltas after backfill.
- [AppViewLite](https://github.com/alnkesq/AppViewLite): adopt durable cursor checkpoints and idempotent reprocessing.
- [Hydrant writeup](https://90008.leaflet.pub/3mhp3t4kuw22e): adopt fairness-focused backfill scheduling and host-aware throttling.
- [Konbini](https://github.com/whyrusleeping/konbini): adopt relevance-scoped indexing for partial/shared mode.

## Implementation Changes
- Create a new standalone AppView runtime target with 3 planes: ingest, indexing/materialization, query API.
- Reuse existing firehose primitives and refactor shared decoding pieces out of SubscribeReposHandler.m.
- Persist durable global cursor and raw event envelopes; enforce idempotency by `(repo, rev/cid)` so replay/backfill/live can safely overlap.
- Add a backfill orchestrator with:
- queue sources: startup sweep, ingest gap detection, admin enqueue, on-demand miss enqueue.
- scheduler: global worker cap + per-host cap + exponential backoff from 429/rate-limit headers.
- worker flow: `getRepo(did, since)` → CAR parse → lexicon-validated apply → state transition.
- repo state machine: `pending`, `processing`, `synced`, `dirty`; live deltas for `processing` repos go to a per-repo pending queue and are merged post-backfill.
- Expand indexers for broad `app.bsky.*` surface (actor/profile, follows/blocks, posts/replies, likes/reposts, lists/listitems, feed generators, notifications, bookmarks) with a dead-letter table for invalid records.
- Implement interest-graph partial mode:
- maintain relevance set `R` from seed DIDs, follows of seeds, explicit allowlist, and recent interaction expansion with TTL.
- ingest full network into raw log; materialize heavy views only when event touches `R`.
- on non-`R` query misses, enqueue async expansion/backfill; optional temporary proxy fallback when enabled.
- Fix local lifecycle wiring risk by removing ephemeral observer ownership in XrpcAppBskyMethods.m and making standalone/proxy behavior explicit.
- Keep using existing durable event storage patterns from ServiceDatabases.m, but split AppView-specific checkpoint/backfill tables into an appview-owned schema.

## Public APIs / Interfaces / Types
- New config contract:
- `appview.mode = standalone|proxy`
- `appview.relay_urls[]`
- `appview.cursor.checkpoint_interval_ms`
- `appview.backfill.enabled`, `appview.backfill.global_workers`, `appview.backfill.per_host_workers`
- `appview.partial.enabled`, `appview.partial.seed_dids[]`, `appview.partial.allowlist[]`, `appview.partial.ttl_hours`, `appview.partial.proxy_fallback`
- New internal admin endpoints:
- `POST /admin/backfill/repos` (enqueue DIDs)
- `POST /admin/backfill/scope/rebuild` (recompute relevance set)
- `GET /admin/backfill/status` (queue depth, worker health, lag)
- New internal types:
- `AppViewCheckpoint` (global seq + timestamp)
- `RepoSyncState` (`did`, `state`, `last_rev`, `last_backfill_at`, `error_count`)
- `PendingRepoDelta` (queued live ops while backfill in-flight)
- `RelevanceMembership` (`did`, `reason`, `expires_at`)

## Test Plan
- Unit tests:
- cursor resume and duplicate suppression across reconnect/replay.
- backfill scheduler fairness (global/per-host caps) and rate-limit backoff behavior.
- repo state transitions and merge correctness for live-during-backfill races.
- relevance membership updates and TTL expiry.
- Integration tests:
- end-to-end ingest from `subscribeRepos` with intentional disconnect/reconnect and zero-loss cursor recovery.
- mixed live + backfill on same repos, asserting eventual parity of materialized state.
- broad `app.bsky.*` query fixtures across posts, graph, notifications, lists, and feeds.
- partial mode query behavior: in-scope hit, out-of-scope miss + enqueue, optional proxy fallback path.
- Soak/perf checks:
- sustained ingest with controlled backfill load, bounded lag, bounded DB growth, no duplicate materialization.

## Assumptions and Defaults
- Chosen by you: standalone runtime, full-network ingest, native `getRepo` backfill, `subscribeRepos` realtime source, broad `app.bsky` API target, interest-graph partial mode.
- v1 uses single-writer global ingest with hash-partitioned workers; true multi-node shard ownership is deferred.
- v1 does not require external Tap service; we keep an adapter seam so Tap/Jetstream can be added later without redesign.
- PDS keeps secure production defaults unchanged; only AppView routing/mode changes during rollout."}

nodes.title: Multi-Service Ops UX (PDS, Relay, AppView, PLC)
nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"main","confidence":90,"prompt":"Build four separate operator-facing web apps on subdomains (pds-ops, relay-ops, appview-ops, plc-ops), with per-service authentication, guarded write actions, and shared interaction model.

Target: ops/admin audience, internet-exposed behind auth
Implementation order: PDS → Relay → AppView → PLC

Key UX decisions:
- Consistent top bar with service switcher, environment badge, auth state, incident count
- Shared IA pattern: Overview, Operations, Investigate, Audit per app
- Dense operator UI with keyboard navigation, timestamps, copyable IDs
- Real-time refresh with staleness indicator
- Guarded writes: preflight summary, risk badge, typed confirmation, reason field, execution receipt with audit ID

API changes required:
1. Relay API parity: POST/DELETE /api/relay/upstreams/*, connect/disconnect endpoints, capability endpoint
2. AppView operator APIs: queue/detail/retry/cancel endpoints, ingest health endpoint, login/session UX
3. PLC auth hardening: per-service auth middleware, structured error codes
4. PDS admin UX: normalize response shapes, audit receipts with correlation ID

Delivery phases:
1. PDS app extraction and hardening (standalone shell, guarded writes, audit)
2. Relay app (overview, upstreams, event stream, host/repo state, mutation endpoints)
3. AppView app (backfill queue, relevance scope, ingest health, session UX)
4. PLC app (directory, detail, timeline, metrics, write operations, auth)"}

nodes.title: Implement relay mutation endpoints (POST/DELETE /api/relay/upstreams, connect/disconnect, capability)

nodes.title: Bsky Datetime Lexicon Conformance
nodes.description: Implement Bsky datetime lexicon compliance following TypeScript and Go reference implementations
nodes.metadata_json: {"branch":"main","confidence":90,"prompt":"Reference implementations analysis:\\n- TypeScript: packages/syntax/src/datetime.ts uses regex + new Date() for semantic validation\\n- Go: atproto/syntax/datetime.go uses regex + time.Parse() for semantic validation\\n\\nRequirements:\\n- Year: 0000-9999 (4 digits)\\n- Month: 01-12\\n- Day: 01-31 (valid for month)\\n- Hour: 00-23\\n- Minute: 00-59\\n- Second: 00-60 (leap seconds per RFC 3339)\\n- Fractional: 1-20 digits\\n- Timezone: Z or ±HH:MM (not -00:00)\\n- Max length: 64 chars\\n- Separator: Uppercase T required\\n\\nCross-platform: Must work on macOS (NSISO8601DateFormatter) and GNUstep (manual sscanf parsing)"}

nodes.title: Update NSDateFormatter+ATProto.m with runtime NSISO8601DateFormatter detection
nodes.description: Add runtime detection for NSISO8601DateFormatter using NSClassFromString. If available (macOS 10.12+, GNUstep 1.27+ with ICU), use native formatter. Otherwise fall back to manual sscanf parsing.

nodes.title: Add validateDatetime: to ATProtoValidator
nodes.description: Add +validateDatetime:error: method to ATProtoValidator.h and implement in ATProtoValidator.m with strict sscanf-based validation matching TypeScript/Go: length check (max 64), reject -00:00, regex pattern, semantic validation via NSDate parsing

nodes.title: Replace direct NSISO8601DateFormatter usage with category methods
nodes.description: Update AppViewDatabase.m and SubscribeReposHandler.m to use [NSDateFormatter atproto_stringFromDate:] and [NSDateFormatter atproto_dateFromString:] instead of direct NSISO8601DateFormatter usage

nodes.title: Verify ATProtoDateTime.m GNUstep fallback works
nodes.description: Ensure manual sscanf-based fallback parsing in ATProtoDateTime.m works correctly on both platforms without NSISO8601DateFormatter

nodes.title: Datetime validation strategy
nodes.description: Use runtime detection + sscanf fallback for cross-platform compatibility, matching TypeScript/Go semantics
nodes.metadata_json: {"branch":"main","confidence":95,"prompt":"Key design choices from reference implementations:\\n\\n1. Validation approach:\\n- TypeScript: regex + new Date() for semantic validation\\n- Go: regex + time.Parse() for semantic validation\\n- Our choice: NSClassFromString detection + sscanf for format + NSDate parsing for semantics\\n\\n2. Leap seconds:\\n- Reference allows 60 (RFC 3339)\\n- Our choice: Allow 60\\n\\n3. Fractional digits:\\n- Reference allows 1-20 (Go) or 1-9+ (TypeScript tests)\\n- Our choice: Allow up to 20\\n\\n4. Year range:\\n- Reference: 0000-9999\\n- Our choice: Same (reject negative years, years > 9999)\\n\\n5. Timezone:\\n- Reference: Z or ±HH:MM, reject -00:00\\n- Our choice: Same"}

nodes.title: Use NSClassFromString runtime detection for NSISO8601DateFormatter availability
nodes.description: Check if NSISO8601DateFormatter class exists at runtime. If yes (macOS 10.12+, GNUstep 1.27+ with ICU), use it. If not, fall back to manual sscanf parsing.
